skill.name: kpi-dashboard-design
skill.description: Design effective KPI dashboards with metrics selection, visualization best practices, and real-time monitoring patterns. Use when building business dashboards, selecting metrics, or designing data ...
skill.summary: Design effective KPI dashboards with metrics selection, visualization best practices, and real-time ...
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All static findings are false positives. The skill contains markdown documentation with code examples (SQL, Python, YAML). The backtick characters flagged as 'external_commands' are markdown syntax for code blocks, not shell execution. External URLs are reference links to educational resources (dashboard design articles), not network requests. No actual security risks identified.

low_findings.title: False Positive: External Command Detection
low_findings.description: Backtick characters in markdown documentation were misidentified as Ruby/shell command execution. These are code block delimiters in the SKILL.md file, not actual execution.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Network Request Detection
low_findings.description: Reference URLs to external educational resources were misidentified as network requests made by the skill.

dangerous_patterns.title: Backtick Characters Misidentified as Command Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The scanner detected backtick characters (`) as Ruby/shell command execution. These are actually markdown syntax for code blocks in the SKILL.md documentation file. No actual shell commands are executed by this skill.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Reference Links Misidentified as Network Requests
dangerous_patterns.description: The scanner detected URLs at lines 440-442 as network requests. These are markdown hyperlinks to external reference resources (Stephen Few's Dashboard Design, Edward Tufte's Principles, Google Data Studio Gallery). They are informational reference links, not network requests made by any code.

dangerous_patterns.title: Block Characters Misidentified as Windows SAM Database
dangerous_patterns.description: The scanner incorrectly identified block characters (█) at line 226 as 'Windows SAM database'. This is a FALSE POSITIVE - the characters are ASCII block art for dashboard visualization in the markdown documentation.

dangerous_patterns.title: SQL Date Functions Misidentified as System Reconnaissance
dangerous_patterns.description: The scanner detected SQL functions DATE_TRUNC and EXTRACT at lines 296 and 310 as 'system reconnaissance'. These are legitimate SQL date manipulation functions used in KPI calculations, not reconnaissance tools.

content.user_title: Design KPI Dashboards That Drive Decisions
content.value_statement: Create effective KPI dashboards that clearly communicate business performance. This skill provides frameworks, visualization patterns, and SQL templates for building executive, departmental, and operational dashboards.
actual_capabilities: Apply KPI framework (strategic, tactical, operational levels) to organize metrics
Design dashboard layouts with executive summary, department views, and drill-downs
Select appropriate KPIs by department (sales, marketing, product, finance)
Create SQL queries for common KPI calculations (MRR, ARR, retention, CAC)
Implement real-time monitoring displays with alerts and thresholds
Follow visualization best practices (color, spacing, chart selection)
limitations: Does not generate full working code (provides patterns and examples only)
Does not connect to specific data sources or databases
Does not provide deployment or infrastructure guidance
Does not cover advanced ML-based forecasting or anomaly detection

use_cases.title: Build Executive Dashboard
use_cases.description: Design a high-level executive summary showing 4-6 headline KPIs with trends and alerts for C-suite stakeholders.
use_cases.target_user: Business analyst or data team lead

use_cases.title: Create Department-Specific Views
use_cases.description: Develop tailored dashboards for sales, marketing, product, or finance teams with relevant metrics and visualizations.

use_cases.title: Implement Real-Time Operations Monitor
use_cases.description: Build an operations center dashboard showing system health, throughput, error rates, and live alerts.

prompt_templates.title: Get KPI Framework
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me understand the KPI framework for dashboard design. I need to know the three levels (strategic, tactical, operational) and when to use each.
prompt_templates.scenario: Understanding the foundational framework before designing a dashboard

prompt_templates.title: Select Sales KPIs
prompt_templates.prompt: What are the most important KPIs for a sales dashboard? Include revenue metrics, pipeline metrics, and activity metrics with brief explanations.

prompt_templates.title: Design Executive Summary Layout
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me a layout pattern for an executive summary dashboard. Include suggested KPIs, visualization types, and how to present trends and alerts.

prompt_templates.title: Write MRR SQL Query
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a SQL query to calculate Monthly Recurring Revenue (MRR) from a subscriptions table. Include month-over-month growth calculation.
prompt_templates.scenario: Implementing KPI calculations in a database

output_examples.input: What KPIs should I include for a SaaS product dashboard?
output: Daily/Monthly Active Users (DAU/MAU) - measure product engagement
Net Promoter Score (NPS) - track customer satisfaction
Customer Acquisition Cost (CAC) - track acquisition efficiency
LTV/CAC Ratio - measure ROI on customer acquisition

output_examples.input: Show me a dashboard layout pattern for executive reporting
output: Top row: 4-6 headline KPI cards with current value and trend indicator
Middle section: Two main charts - trend over time and breakdown by segment
Bottom section: Alert banner for critical issues and recommended actions

best_practices: Limit the dashboard to 5-7 KPIs to avoid overwhelming the audience
Show context with comparisons (previous period, target, benchmark)
Use consistent color coding: green for positive trends, red for negative
anti_patterns: Including vanity metrics that look impressive but do not drive decisions
Overcrowding the dashboard with too many charts and tables
Using 3D charts or decorative elements that distort data perception

faq.question: What is the difference between strategic, tactical, and operational KPIs?
faq.answer: Strategic KPIs track long-term goals (monthly/quarterly) for executives. Tactical KPIs measure department goals (weekly/monthly) for managers. Operational KPIs monitor day-to-day activities (real-time/daily) for teams.

faq.question: How many KPIs should be on a single dashboard?
faq.answer: Aim for 5-7 KPIs on any single view. Executive summaries should be even more concise (4-6). Use drill-downs for detailed analysis.

faq.question: What are SMART KPIs?
faq.answer: SMART stands for Specific (clear definition), Measurable (quantifiable), Achievable (realistic targets), Relevant (aligned to goals), and Time-bound (defined period).

faq.question: How often should dashboard KPIs be updated?
faq.answer: Match update frequency to the KPI type: operational (real-time/daily), tactical (weekly/monthly), strategic (monthly/quarterly). Do not refresh faster than data accuracy allows.

faq.question: What is a good LTV/CAC ratio?
faq.answer: A healthy LTV/CAC ratio is typically 3:1 or higher. This means customer lifetime value is at least three times the cost to acquire them.

faq.question: How do I choose the right chart type for my KPI?
faq.answer: Use line charts for trends over time. Use bar charts for comparisons across categories. Use pie charts for part-to-whole (limited slices). Use gauges for current status against targets.
